name: phase7-real-world-compatibility
description: Release-facing index of real-world Docker Compose patterns covered by the current Susun compatibility corpus.

patterns.title: Single service config, analysis, and dry-run planning
patterns.evidence: Covers the smallest useful application shape for SDK consumers and Studio project import.

patterns.title: Image build context with Dockerfile, args, cache hints, SSH, and secret identities
patterns.evidence: Covers neutral build input modeling, dockerignore handling, and redacted build secret identities.
deferred: Full remote BuildKit parity and registry credential flows remain release-gated future work.

patterns.title: File-backed Compose configs and secrets
patterns.evidence: Covers config and secret resource modeling without exposing secret file contents in compatibility artifacts.
deferred: External secret providers are intentionally outside the v0.1.0 supported subset.

patterns.title: Multi-file projects with runtime and convergence planning
patterns.evidence: Covers override files, ports, volumes, dependencies, runtime command inputs, and convergence planning evidence.
deferred: Runtime parity still depends on Docker integration checks and is not claimed for every Compose edge case.

patterns.title: Develop/watch restart and sync workflows
patterns.evidence: Covers the initial watch/develop model used by desktop tooling experiments.
deferred: Bidirectional sync, conflict handling, and platform-specific file event parity remain future work.

patterns.title: Include, extends, and YAML merge-tag loading
patterns.evidence: Covers advanced loading metadata and parser behavior without treating every Compose extension as stable.
deferred: Remote includes and undocumented Docker Compose loader quirks remain explicitly deferred.

patterns.title: Profiled web application stack with database dependency
patterns.evidence: Covers a more realistic Studio import target with active/default services, profiled dev service, service_healthy dependency, healthchecks, restart policy, labels, named volumes, custom networks, host-bound port, and environment interpolation.
deferred: Runtime health behavior and database readiness are modeled for planning evidence but are not executed as part of this fixture.
